---
title: "AGENTS.md, {admiral}, and the AI-Assisted Programmer"
author:
  - name: Arianna Cascone
  - name: Jeff Dickinson
description: ""
# Note that the date below will be auto-updated when the post is merged.
date: "2026-03-20"
# Please do not use any non-default categories.
# You can find the default categories in the repository README.md
categories: [Technical, ADaM, Community]
# Feel free to change the image
image: "agents_md_admiral.png"

---

<!--------------- typical setup ----------------->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
long_slug <- "2026-03-20-agents-md-admiral-a"
```

<!--------------- post begins here ----------------->

## Introduction

<!-- Introduce the concept of AGENTS.md and how it fits into AI-assisted development workflows. -->

## What is AGENTS.md?

<!-- Explain what AGENTS.md is: a plain-text file that gives AI coding agents (like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, etc.) project-specific instructions — where to find tests, coding conventions, which functions to prefer, etc. -->

## Why admiral?

<!-- Describe the admiral package and why it is a good fit for this kind of AI-guided workflow: consistent API, well-documented functions, pharmaverse conventions. -->

## Writing an AGENTS.md for admiral

<!-- Walk through what to include in an AGENTS.md tailored to an admiral-based ADaM project: preferred functions, test setup, style requirements, CDISC naming conventions, etc. -->

## The AI-Assisted Programming Experience

<!-- Share the experience of using an AI agent (e.g. GitHub Copilot / Claude) with the AGENTS.md in place. What worked well? Where did the agent need guiding? -->

## Lessons Learned

<!-- Key takeaways: what made the AGENTS.md effective, what to avoid, and recommendations for other pharmaverse developers. -->

## Conclusion

<!-- Wrap up and point readers to the admiral documentation and pharmaverse resources. -->

<!--------------- appendices go here ----------------->

```{r, echo=FALSE}
source("appendix.R")
insert_appendix(
  repo_spec = "pharmaverse/blog",
  name = long_slug,
  # file_name should be the name of your file
  file_name = list.files() %>% stringr::str_subset(".qmd") %>% first()
)
```
